critical.mandate: Append validation results to the story file
Read the story template from {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/templates/story-template.md for reference

step.title: Load Story
step.ask: Which story to validate? (provide story key, e.g., 1.2)
step.action: Find and read the story file matching {story_key}-*.md in {implementation_artifacts}/

step.title: Header Metadata
step.action: Verify header metadata is present and populated: Epic, Sprint, Date, Author, Status, Traces to

step.title: Semantic Quality Checks
step.action: Duplicate detection: load all story files for the same epic from {implementation_artifacts}/. Compare titles and acceptance criteria scope for overlap. Flag any stories with >70% AC overlap as potential duplicates.

Architecture cross-reference: load {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md. For each ADR referenced in the story, verify the ADR exists and its status is Accepted. Flag references to Proposed or Deprecated ADRs as warnings. Flag story approaches that conflict with architecture decisions as errors.
Report findings even if clean: "No duplicate stories found in epic" / "All ADR references valid"

step.title: Completeness
step.action: Verify all sections present: User Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Technical Notes, Dependencies, Test Scenarios, Project Structure Notes, References, Dev Agent Record, Estimate, Definition of Done

step.title: Clarity
step.action: Check each AC is testable and unambiguous (Given/When/Then format)
Verify subtasks are actionable and linked to AC numbers
Verify Definition of Done items are specific and measurable

step.title: Dependencies
step.action: Verify all declared dependencies exist and are resolvable

step.title: Generate Validation Report
step.action: Append validation result at the END of the story file, after a horizontal rule (---) separator

Also create a separate validation report file at {implementation_artifacts}/story-validation-{story_key}.md with YAML frontmatter: --- story_key: "{story_key}" validation_status: "PASS" or "FAIL" validated_at: "{ISO-8601 timestamp}" findings: - section: "{section name}" result: "PASS" or "FAIL" issue: "{description if FAIL}" --- Then detailed findings for each section.
step.template-output: Append a section titled "## Validation Report" at the very end of the story file, preceded by a --- separator. Include: date, overall PASS/FAIL verdict, and per-section findings (PASS or FAIL with specific issue description). This report will be read by /gaia-fix-story to resolve issues.

Create standalone validation report with YAML frontmatter (story_key, validation_status, validated_at, findings array) and detailed per-section results. This file is the machine-readable validation artifact for downstream automation.

step.title: Update Story Status
step.action: If overall verdict is PASS: update story status to 'ready-for-dev' in the story file header and in {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml
If overall verdict is FAIL: keep story status as 'validating' — instruct user to run /gaia-fix-story then re-run /gaia-validate-story
step.template-output: Update sprint-status.yaml with the story status: ready-for-dev (if PASS) or validating (if FAIL).
